conjecture_title: Open question on existence of lim n(2,d)/d^2
paper_title: New bounds on the maximum number of neighborly boxes in R^d
summary: The question of whether $\lim_{d\to\infty} n(2,d)/d^2$ exists remains open. The source paper establishes the lower bound $n(2,d) > (1-o(1))d^2/2$ and conjectures the limit equals $1/2$ if it exists. A 2024 follow-up (arXiv:2402.02199) gives new lower bounds via a recursive construction of 2-neighborly codes and proposes a conjectured explicit formula for $n(2,d)$, but explicitly acknowledges that the existence of the limit is still unknown. No paper resolving the existence question was found.

since_posted.title: Neighborly boxes and bipartite coverings; constructions and conjectures
since_posted.claim: Provides improved lower bounds on $n(2,d)$ via a recursive construction of k-neighborly codes and conjectures an explicit formula for $n(2,d)$, while explicitly noting that the existence of $\lim_{d\to\infty} n(2,d)/d^2$ remains unknown.

search_queries: neighborly boxes n(2,d) limit d^2 Alon Grytczuk Kisielewicz Przesławski arXiv 2212.05133
neighborly boxes R^d maximum number n(2,d) asymptotic 2023 2024 2025
notes: A 2024 follow-up (arXiv:2402.02199) explicitly restates the open question, confirming the limit question was unresolved as of February 2024. Authors of arXiv:2402.02199 were not retrieved from the abstract fetch (PDF was not machine-readable). Confidence is medium rather than high because the conjecture is now ~3 years old and further progress since 2024 could not be ruled out within the 5-call cap.
